content: The `enos-webhook` ingress tunnel was successfully transitioned from a PM2-managed process to a native Windows background service. This critical change ensures the tunnel starts automatically on system boot, independent of user sessions, thereby fulfilling the Ingress Resilience requirement for Epic #99 and preventing service interruptions.
one_liner: Successfully migrated Cloudflare ingress tunnel to a resilient Windows service for automatic startup and enhanced system resilience.
source_context: implementation_plan.md: 'This plan transitions the `enos-webhook` ingress tunnel from a PM2-managed process to a native Windows background service.'

content: The Cloudflare ingress tunnel is now exclusively managed by the Windows Service Control Manager (`services.msc` or `sc.exe`). Agents must no longer attempt to manage or monitor the `enos-ingress-tunnel` via PM2, as it has been removed from PM2's process list.
one_liner: Manage Cloudflare tunnel via Windows Service Control Manager, not PM2.
source_context: walkthrough.md: 'The tunnel is now managed by the Windows Service Control Manager. You will no longer see it in `pm2 list`.'

content: The `setup_ingress.ps1` script is now responsible for registering `cloudflared.exe` as a native Windows system service, configured for automatic startup. This ensures the Cloudflare agent is always active and bound to the correct tunnel token, providing persistent ingress.
one_liner: Cloudflared agent installed as an automatic Windows service via `setup_ingress.ps1`.
source_context: implementation_plan.md: 'Run the script with the retrieved Tunnel Token to install the "Cloudflared agent" service.'

content: End-to-end connectivity for the `enos-webhook` ingress tunnel was successfully verified by hitting the `https://hooks.mechanistic.com/health` endpoint, which returned an 'online' status. This confirms the Cloudflared agent service is correctly routing traffic.
one_liner: End-to-end connectivity for the Cloudflare ingress tunnel successfully verified.
source_context: walkthrough.md: 'Validated end-to-end connectivity by hitting the health endpoint: `https://hooks.mechanistic.com/health`.'

content: It was clarified that the `nanoclaw_enabled` status, observed in the health endpoint response, is controlled by the `.env` file and is entirely unrelated to the operational status or connectivity of the Cloudflare ingress tunnel. This distinction is important for debugging and configuration understanding.
one_liner: `nanoclaw_enabled` status is independent of Cloudflare tunnel connectivity.
source_context: walkthrough.md: '`nanoclaw_enabled` is currently `false` in the heartbeat. This is controlled by the `.env` file and is unrelated to the tunnel connectivity.'
